page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-32 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the x86_64 AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 16 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading enabled. The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local NVMe storage or bundled GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage options.

In performance evaluations, the AMD EPYC processor demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, scoring in the top 25% on stress-ng benchmarks. Multi-core performance and memory bandwidth tests fall within the average tier, with uncached large-block read and write speeds reaching 89.01 GB/sec and 52.3 GB/sec respectively. Cryptographic and integer operations are supported by hardware-accelerated instruction sets including AES, VAES, and SHA_NI. The instance achieves average throughput in database operations and static web serving, processing 31.49 million Redis SET operations per second and delivering 23.71 GB/sec of web serving throughput.

The n2d-standard-32 offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose workloads. While it handles small to medium language model inference adequately, performance drops significantly on larger models, as evidenced by a weak prompt processing speed of 6.55 tokens/sec on a 70B model. This indicates a clear tradeoff for memory-intensive AI workloads. The instance is highly suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database hosting, and high-throughput web serving where a balanced vCPU-to-RAM ratio is required without the need for local SSDs or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-32 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 16 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading and a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth results, including uncached read speeds of 89.01 GB/sec. The instance supports hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES and SHA_NI. It is well suited for general-purpose workloads such as database operations, software compilation, and static web serving, though it exhibits performance trade-offs under large-scale LLM inference tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-32 is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, delivering balanced performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-32 features 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, optimized for general-purpose workloads and database operations.
tagline: A balanced x86_64 virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and 128 GB RAM for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
128.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU
Strong single-core CPU performance in stress-ng benchmarks
Hardware-accelerated cryptography with AES, VAES, and SHA_NI support
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and web serving
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the base configuration
